name: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)
source: 45 CFR Parts 160 and 164 (Privacy, Security and Breach Notification Rules)
description: US federal rules protecting individually identifiable health information (PHI) held by covered entities and business associates. Encoded at rule/safeguard level; implementation specifications are summarised.

principles.title: Permitted uses and disclosures of PHI
principles.summary: PHI may only be used or disclosed as permitted or required, such as for treatment, payment, and health care operations, or with authorisation.
queries: when health information may be used or disclosed
authorisation for disclosure of health records
principles.expects: The document limits use and disclosure of health information to permitted purposes or authorised cases.

principles.title: Minimum necessary standard
principles.summary: Uses, disclosures, and requests of PHI must be limited to the minimum necessary to accomplish the intended purpose.
queries: minimum necessary access to health information
limiting staff access to patient records
principles.expects: The document restricts access to and disclosure of health information to the minimum necessary.

principles.title: Business associate agreements
principles.summary: Disclosures to business associates require contracts obligating them to safeguard PHI.
queries: business associate agreement
third party vendors handling health information
principles.expects: The document requires agreements with vendors that handle health information.

principles.title: Notice of privacy practices
principles.summary: Individuals are entitled to a notice describing how PHI is used and disclosed and their rights.
queries: notice of privacy practices
informing patients how their information is used
principles.expects: The document provides or references a privacy notice for patients.

principles.title: Individual right of access
principles.summary: Individuals have a right to inspect and obtain a copy of their PHI in a designated record set.
queries: patient access to their own records
requesting copies of health information
principles.expects: The document lets individuals access and obtain copies of their health information.

principles.title: Right to amendment
principles.summary: Individuals may request amendment of inaccurate or incomplete PHI, with a process for accepting or denying requests.
queries: correcting or amending health records
disputing inaccurate patient information
principles.expects: The document provides a process to amend inaccurate health information.

principles.title: Administrative safeguards
principles.summary: Security management processes including risk analysis, workforce training, access management, and contingency planning.
queries: security risk analysis and management
workforce security training and access management
principles.expects: The document covers risk assessment, staff training and administrative security processes.

principles.title: Physical safeguards
principles.summary: Facility access controls, workstation security, and device and media controls for systems holding ePHI.
queries: physical access controls to facilities and workstations
device and media disposal controls
principles.expects: The document addresses physical protection of facilities, workstations and media.

principles.title: Technical safeguards
principles.summary: Access control, audit controls, integrity protections, authentication, and transmission security for ePHI.
queries: technical access controls audit logs authentication
encryption of health data in transit
principles.expects: The document describes technical controls such as access control, audit logging, and encryption.

principles.title: Breach notification
principles.summary: Following a breach of unsecured PHI, affected individuals, HHS, and in some cases the media must be notified within required timeframes.
queries: breach notification to individuals and regulators
responding to a breach of health information
principles.expects: The document sets out breach response and notification obligations with timeframes.
